title: GST
lead: Implemented in ==2017== — India's boldest step towards ==‘one nation, one tax, one market’==. The PAC's ==19th report== is scathing about how the regime has delivered on simplification and compliance.

items.label: essentials — household goods, packed food, clothing, shoes, pharma
new standard rate for most goods, services, electronics — replacing 28%
luxury/sin — luxury cars, high-end motorcycles, pan masala, tobacco, aerated drinks
health and life insurance premiums — now exempt

items.title: Advantages
bullets: ==No cascading== + input tax credit → lower tax burden

items.title: Issues
bullets: ==Tech and compliance hurdles== for SMEs with limited digital literacy
PAC: ==~2 percentage-point drop== in indirect tax revenue between FY18 and FY20 (pre-COVID)
==Centralising tendencies==: destination-based levy hurts ==manufacturing-heavy states==; fiscal autonomy concerns
==Compensation delays== — the promised 14% annual growth (2017–22, FY16 base) often arrived late or not at all
==Exclusions==: alcohol and select petroleum products remain outside GST

blocks.text: GST was envisioned as a unifying force for India's fragmented tax landscape — but unity without equity breeds friction. The real test of GST lies not in its design, but in its delivery. For GST to truly succeed, it must balance efficiency with empathy, and harmonisation with federal trust.
